basmala: In the Name of God, the Infinitely Merciful, the Most Merciful.
intro: Al-Zalzala — “The Earthquake” — is placed by some at the end of the Meccan period, by others in the early Medinan period (around year 1 of the Hijra). Its name comes from the verb zalzala (زَلْزَلَ), “to shake violently”, “to convulse”. Five verses describe the trembling of the earth on the Day of Judgment, its burdens disgorged, man's astonishment, and the very voice of the earth become a witness. The last three verses set out the lesson: every deed, even a mote in weight, will be seen. According to a saying attributed to Ibn ʿAbbās, “idhā zulzilat equals half the Quran” — for the sūra condenses everything the Book says about the Hereafter. A ḥadīth adds that an old man, unable to retain longer sūras, came to the Prophet asking for a brief word that would contain it all: it was al-Zalzala that he was given.

versets.trad: When the earth is shaken with her shaking,
versets.commentaire: zulzilat (زُلْزِلَتْ): passive of the verb zalzala (زَلْزَلَ), an intensive reduplication of the root z-l, “to shake”. The verb itself trembles — its form mimics the thing. zilzālahā (زِلْزَالَهَا), “her own shaking”, adds the possessive: each thing will tremble with its own shaking. The earth, which seemed the most stable thing, will be touched at its heart.

mots.def: temporal particle introducing the circumstance: when, at the moment that.
zalzala: to shake violently; in the passive perfect feminine, subject: the earth.
mots.gram: passive perfect verb, 3rd pers. fem. sing.

mots.def: arḍ: the earth, the ground, the planet.
zilzāl: verbal noun of zalzala, “the shaking”, “the quaking”; with feminine suffix pronoun: her own shaking.

versets.trad: and the earth casts forth her burdens,
versets.commentaire: athqāl (أَثْقَال): the burdens, what she carries that weighs — the dead, the buried treasures, what men have hidden. A ḥadīth describes the moment: “The earth will throw out its entrails; gold and silver will come out like columns. The murderer will come and say, ‘For this I killed!' The one who broke kinship will say, ‘For this I severed!' The thief will say, ‘For this my hands were cut!' Then they will leave it there, and none will take anything from it.”

mots.def: conjunction: and.
akhraja: to bring out, to expel, to yield up; feminine perfect.
arḍ: the earth.
athqāl: plural of thiql, burden, load; what the earth carries that weighs, including her dead and her treasures.

versets.trad: and man cries out: “What ails her?”
versets.commentaire: mā lahā (مَا لَهَا): “what ails her?”, “what is the matter with her?”. The common expression for astonishment in face of an unexpected event. According to some, the question is that of all human beings; according to others, only of the deniers, since the believers already knew this world was passing. Either way, astonishment is the first attitude: the earth, the foundation of every habit, breaks its habit.

mots.def: conjunction: and.
qāla: to say; perfect with future sense in the circumstance.
insān: man, the human being.
interrogative: what?
preposition li- + feminine suffix pronoun: to her, for her.

versets.trad: That Day she shall convey her chronicles,
versets.commentaire: tuḥaddithu akhbārahā (تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا): “she will tell her news”. The verb ḥaddatha (حَدَّثَ), intensive of ḥadatha, names the conveying of a narrative — the same verb that gives ḥadīth, the words reported from the Prophet. The earth becomes a narrator. According to a ḥadīth: “Her chronicles means that she will testify against every male and female: on such a day, so-and-so did such a thing on her surface. That is her chronicle.” Nothing done upon the soil has escaped the soil.

mots.def: yawm + idh: that day, on that very day.
ḥaddatha: intensive of ḥadatha (to happen, to occur); to tell, to convey a narrative. Same root as ḥadīth.
akhbār: plural of khabar, news, account, chronicle; with feminine suffix pronoun: her news.

versets.trad: for your Lord shall have inspired her.
versets.commentaire: awḥā (أَوْحَىٰ): to inspire, to suggest, to communicate a subtle command — this is the verb used for prophetic revelation (waḥy), but also for what God “inspires” to the bee (16:68) or to the sky (41:12). Here the earth herself is instructed by God to speak all. The verse thus recalls that the witness coming out of the earth is not her initiative, but a word rendered by divine command.

mots.def: preposition bi-: by; introduces cause or means.
subordinating conjunction: that.
rabb + suffix pronoun: your Lord.
awḥā: to inspire, to communicate subtly, to reveal; same root as waḥy, prophetic revelation.
preposition li- + feminine suffix pronoun.

versets.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِّيُرَوْا أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: yawmaʾidhin yaṣduru n-nāsu ashtātan li-yuraw aʿmālahum
versets.trad: That Day mankind shall come forth in scattered groups, that their deeds may be shown to them.
versets.commentaire: ashtātan (أَشْتَاتًا): plural of shatīt, scattered, separated, each going his way. The word says two things: dispersion (each alone before his deeds), and divergence (some toward light, others elsewhere). The Quran will make it precise: “that Day you shall come to Us alone, as We created you the first time” (6:94). li-yuraw aʿmālahum: “that their deeds may be shown to them” — not only that they may see the result, but that they see the deeds themselves, as they truly were.

mots.def: see v. 4.
ṣadara: to come forth, to issue out, to go forward; imperfect singular (verb before a plural subject).
an-nās: mankind.
ashtāt: plural of shatīt, scattered, separated; here, each going his own way.
li- + yuraw: preposition of purpose + passive subjunctive plural; “that it may be shown to them”.
mots.gram: preposition + passive subjunctive verb, 3rd pers. masc. plur.

mots.def: aʿmāl: plural of ʿamal, deed, work; with plural suffix pronoun.

versets.ar: فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ
versets.tr: fa-man yaʿmal mithqāla dharratin khayran yarah
versets.trad: So whoever has done a mote's weight of good shall see it,
versets.commentaire: mithqāl dharra (مِثْقَال ذَرَّة): a mote's weight. Dharra (ذَرَّة) names in old Arabic the tiny ant, or the dust seen in a sunbeam — the smallest unit conceivable. Elsewhere the Quran restates: “not a mote's weight escapes Him” (34:3). An early commentator reports: one man would refuse to give a beggar a single date or a bit of bread, saying: “Too little, I will be rewarded only for what costs me.” The other made light of small sins. The sūra was revealed for both at once.

mots.def: linking particle.
relative/conditional pronoun: whoever.
ʿamila: to do, to act; apocopated imperfect after the conditional.
mots.gram: apocopated imperfect verb, 3rd pers. masc. sing.

mots.def: mithqāl: weight, measure; in the construct accusative: “the weight of”.
dharra: in old Arabic, the smallest thing conceivable — the tiny ant or dust in a sunbeam; today rendered as “atom”.
khayr: the good; indefinite accusative.
raʾā: to see; apocopated imperfect + suffix pronoun.
mots.gram: apocopated imperfect + suffix pronoun, 3rd pers. masc. sing.

versets.ar: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ
versets.tr: wa-man yaʿmal mithqāla dharratin sharran yarah
versets.trad: and whoever has done a mote's weight of evil shall see it.
versets.commentaire: The symmetry is exact: everything, in truth, is seen — good and evil alike, in their smallest measure. ʿĀʾisha reports a saying of the Prophet: “O ʿĀʾisha, beware of the sins that are belittled — for indeed they will be reckoned by God.” And another: “Fear the Fire, even by giving half a date in charity, even by saying one word of good.” The sūra dissolves the illusion that what is small has no weight: no deed is too slight to escape the measure.

mots.def: conjunction: and.
relative/conditional pronoun.
ʿamila: to do.
mots.gram: apocopated imperfect verb, 3rd pers. masc. sing.

mots.def: mithqāl: see v. 7.
dharra: see v. 7.
sharr: evil; indefinite accusative.
raʾā: to see.
mots.gram: apocopated imperfect + suffix pronoun, 3rd pers. masc. sing.
